title: CHEM 109 - General Chemistry I
description: Fundamental concepts: chemical foundations, stoichiometry, chemical reactions, gases, thermochemistry, atomic theory, bonding, liquids, solutions. For students in science, engineering, and related majors.

title: CHEM 110 - General Chemistry II
description: Fundamental concepts: kinetics; equilibrium; acids and bases; acid-base, solubility, and complex ion equilibria; entropy and free energy; electrochemistry; qualitative analysis. For students in science, engineering, and related majors.

title: PHYX 106 - College Physics: Mechanics and Heat
description: Non-Calculus, for science majors. Mechanics, fluids, heat, sound.

title: PHYX 107 - College Physics: Electromagnetism and Modern Physics
description: Non-Calculus, for science majors. Geometric optics, electricity, magnetism, electromagnetic waves, AC circuits, physical optics, relativity.

title: PHYX 109 - General Physics A: Mechanics
description: Calculus-based, for science/engineering students.

title: PHYX 210 - General Physics B: Thermodynamics, Waves and Optics
description: Calculus-based, for science/ engineering students.

title: PHYX 211 - General Physics C: Electricity, Magnetism
description: Calculus-based, for science/ engineering students.

title: MATH 105 - Calculus for the Biological Sciences and Natural Resources
description: Differential and integral calculus. Apply to biological sciences, including exponential growth and decay.

title: MATH 215 - Multivariate Calculus for the Biological Sciences and Natural Resources
description: Differential equations, partial derivatives, double integrals, and curve fitting techniques; vectors; applications.

title: MATH 109 - Calculus I
description: Limits, continuity, derivatives, integrals, and their applications.

title: MATH 110 - Calculus II
description: Logarithmic and exponential functions, inverse trigonometric functions, techniques of integration, infinite sequences and series, conic sections, polar coordinates.

title: MATH 210 - Calculus III
description: Vectors; parametric equations; 3-dimensional analytic geometry; vector- valued functions; partial derivatives; multiple integrals; introduction to line integrals.

title: CHEM 310 - Inorganic Chemistry I
description: Advanced concepts: nuclear properties, molecular symmetry, bonding, metallic and ionic solids, acids and bases, oxidation-reduction, non-aqueous media, chemistry and organometallic compounds of the representative elements.

title: CHEM 323 - Nuclear Magnetic Resonance Spectroscopy (NMR) Techniques
description: Operate NMR spectrometer; prepare samples. Individual projects.

title: CHEM 324 - Organic Chemistry I
description: First semester of a one-year sequence. Chemical bonding, chemical structure, spectroscopy, physical properties, stereochemistry, reaction mechanisms, and synthesis.

title: CHEM 324L - Organic Chemistry I Laboratory
description: First semester of a year-long sequence. Laboratory techniques, library skills, and synthesis.

title: CHEM 325 - Organic Chemistry II
description: Second semester of a one-year sequence. Chemical bonding, chemical structure, spectroscopy, physical properties, stereochemistry, reaction mechanisms, and synthesis.

title: CHEM 325L - Organic Chemistry II Laboratory
description: Second semester of a year-long sequence. Laboratory techniques, spectroscopy, library skills, unknown analysis, and synthesis.

title: CHEM 341 - Quantitative Analysis
description: Principles and methods of classical chemical analysis. Introduction to instrumental methods. For chemistry majors and others who require a rigorous treatment of solution equilibria and training in precise quantitative lab techniques.

title: CHEM 361 - Physical Chemistry I
description: Application of quantitative mathematical methods to fundamental chemical systems: equilibrium thermodynamics and chemical kinetics.

title: CHEM 438 - Introductory Biochemistry
description: Brief course in biochemistry. The chemistry of amino acids, proteins, nucleic acids, lipids and carbohydrates. Includes enzyme kinetics, bioenergetics, structure and function of biological membranes, discussion of common laboratory methods.

title: CHEM 485 - Seminar in Chemistry
description: Seminar presentations on current chemistry topics by majors with senior standing in chemistry. Capstone course. All chemistry majors are encouraged to attend.

title: CHEM 362 - Physical Chemistry II
description: Application of quantitative mathematical methods to fundamental chemical systems: quantum theory, spectroscopy, and statistical thermodynamics.

title: CHEM 363 - Physical Chemistry II Lab
description: Experimental application of quantitative mathematical methods to fundamental chemical systems: laboratory investigations in equilibrium thermodynamics, chemical kinetics, quantum theory, spectroscopy, and statistical thermodynamics.

title: CHEM 410 - Inorganic Chemistry II
description: Advanced concepts: chemistry and organometallic compounds of the transition metals, the lanthanoids, and the actinoids; reaction mechanisms; catalysis; solid state chemistry.

title: CHEM 410L - Inorganic Chemistry II Lab
description: Advanced laboratory and instrumentation techniques: synthesis, characterization, and reactions of inorganic and organometallic compounds.

title: CHEM 441 - Instrumental Analysis
description: Principles and methods. For chemistry majors and others requiring training in instrumental techniques of analysis.

title: CHEM 434 - Biochemistry I
description: First semester lecture of a one-year sequence. Biochemical energetics, introductory metabolism, nature and mechanism of action of enzymes.

title: CHEM 434L - Biochemistry I Laboratory
description: First semester of a one-year sequence. Laboratory techniques.

title: CHEM 435 - Biochemistry II
description: The second semester lecture of a one-year sequence. Biochemical energetics, introductory metabolism, nature and mechanism of action of enzymes.

title: CHEM 435L - Biochemistry II Laboratory
description: Second semester of a one-year lab sequence. Biochemical energetics, introductory metabolism, nature and mechanism of action of enzymes.
